behavior: The installed service answers authenticated /readyz before a release is accepted.
A server restart preserves the tmux session, snapshot, and ability to run the next command.
Closing a terminal requires confirmation, removes exactly that tmux session, and preserves adjacent sessions on cancel or failure.
Raw terminal input reaches the PTY and survives reload, reconnect, and runtime snapshot restoration.
UTF-8 input larger than one WebSocket message arrives once, in order, and byte-exact.
Unacknowledged input is not replayed after a terminal runtime epoch change.
Output and terminal protocol replies from a previous session cannot cross into the selected session.
Native focus reports stay ordered, acknowledged, scoped to the controller, and blocked for spectators.
A pending multiline paste remains bound to its source session and is cancelled after a session switch.
Stopped history never accepts input and starting from it creates a separate live terminal.
Wheel and trackpad gestures move tmux scrollback and never synthesize shell-history arrow keys.
The mobile viewport does not overflow or collapse and all eight terminal keys retain their byte mappings.
One click creates exactly one normal Home-directory terminal without reopening removed option dialogs.
Raw xterm remains the default surface and removed toolbar, preferences, and creation controls stay absent.
Commands and supported interactive programs expose running, finished, and ready states without marking an idle draft busy.
An ordinary Persian draft is joined and its cursor is reconciled at the visual RTL edge.
Moving inside a Persian draft preserves logical text and maps the caret to the mirrored visual column.
Mixed Persian and Latin input with a trailing blank cell retains RTL shaping and cursor alignment.
A legacy private pane with retained history is quarantined and cannot be attached, captured, or written.
Composer V2 is opt-in, raw xterm remains the rollback default, and mixed-script text is sent in logical order exactly once.
Composer draft line breaks and terminal control characters cannot bypass the existing single-command paste-safety policy.
